event: never experienced abdominal pain like this before
CT aortogram confirmed tortuous and aneurysmal dilatation of the abdominal aorta
abdominal aorta measuring up to 10.8 × 10.8 cm in axial dimension
abdominal aorta measuring 24.1 cm in cranio-caudal length extending into the left common iliac artery
proximal aspect involving the origin of the bilateral renal arteries
emergency open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair
aortic cross clamp applied beneath the right renal artery
aortic cross clamp just proximal to left renal artery
no need for renal artery implantation
anastomosed distally to the bilateral common iliac arteries
left lower limb appeared pale intraoperatively
on table angiogram of the left lower limb
abrupt cut-off at the level of the left popliteal artery
transverse arteriotomy over the left common femoral artery
Fogarty balloon catheter used to trawl out multiple thrombi
final angiogram demonstrated in-line flow to the foot via the anterior tibial artery
foot pulses strong at the end of the procedure
transferred to the intensive care unit (ICU)
started on nasogastric feeding on POD2
escalating to oral diet by POD 6
developed a fever on the 4th day of ICU stay
no signs of infection at surgical sites
fit for discharge from hospital by POD 12
histopathological examination revealed degenerative changes of the aortic wall
no evidence of infective process in the aortic wall
mother had significant bilateral hallux valgus
youngest sibling (25-year-old male) with pectus carinatum
no known cardiac disease in the family
no known lung disease in the family
no known rheumatological disease in the family
erythrocyte sedimentation rate elevated at 35 mm/h
no evidence of atheroma on imaging
